name: BS 7671 Appendix 4 — Voltage Drop
formula_reference: BS 7671:2018+A3 Appendix 4, Tables 4Ab–4F; Appendix 12; IEC 60364-5-52 Annex G
implementation_note: Voltage drop = mV/A/m × design_current × length / 1000. The mV/A/m value depends on conductor size, insulation, installation method, and circuit type — requires a table lookup against Appendix 4 Tables 4D1A–4F. Loaded circuits also need power-factor and temperature corrections per Appendix 4 §6. Inline LLM math reliably picks the wrong column (PVC vs XLPE, ref method, single-phase vs 3-phase) on 15–20% of representative cases. Must be tool-executed.

inputs.description: Ib — design current of the circuit
Load PF for correction (1.0 for resistive, ~0.85 typical for motors)
Compliance target: 3% for lighting, 5% for power, 6% for motor starting

vd_percent.description: Voltage drop as % of nominal voltage

table_reference.description: Which Appendix 4 table row was used

runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { vd_mv_per_a_per_m, vd_volts, vd_percent, compliant, table_reference }, error?: string }
